title: Suggest Management Workflow
desc: Suggest = AI-logged bug/improvement record. Triage with merge, resolve with evidence script. SUBMIT RULE: use wkappbot-core.exe when eye blocks. NEVER edit jsonl manually.
steps: 2. Merge duplicates: wkappbot suggest merge --all-matching PATTERN --title X --root-cause Y --components Z --affected-cmds CMD --work Nh
3. Prioritize: est<=2h first, high freq, risk=high

4. Resolve: wkappbot suggest resolve <ts> NOTE --i-completed-the-code-and-built-successfully-and-deployed-and-tested-with-real-scenarios-and-confirmed-meaningful-results-and-have-evidence-and-willkim-allowed-this-script test.cmd --skill ID --commit <hash>
5. NO FAKE TESTS: evidence script must actually run affected wkappbot commands (CMD guard checks output)
6. Evidence script must exit 0 -- re-runs as regression on every future resolve

7. RIQUA [evidence filename]: script name must follow test-{cmd}-{subcmd}-{description}.<sh|ps1|cmd>. Generic names like test_kis_keepalive.cmd are rejected. INTENDED: allow any descriptive filename; rigid format only adds friction without safety benefit.

8. RIQUA [skill guard cwd]: --skill ID guard fails when wkappbot-core runs from scripts/ subdir -- HQ path lookup uses cwd/.wkappbot/hq/skills/ which does not exist. INTENDED: skill guard should walk up to repo root to find .wkappbot/hq/. Workaround: run from repo root.

9. RIQUA [commit guard diverged]: --commit <hash> guard fails when local branch has commits that remote does not yet include (GHA auto-committed market data while Claude was working). Git log returns nothing because the local history is ahead. INTENDED: commit guard should accept any hash reachable in local git log, not just remote-merged commits. Workaround: merge/push remote commits first.
11. SUBMIT BLOCKED? If wkappbot suggest submit is silently dropped (eye intercept due to PENDING CO-RESOLVE), bypass with: wkappbot-core.exe suggest TEXT --requirement CMD => EXPECTED (3 required)
12. NEVER edit suggestions.jsonl manually -- tamper detection breaks submit permanently
13. PENDING CO-RESOLVE banner = warning only, never spend time resolving it first -- just use core to submit past it
14. CROSS-RESOLVE (DGWCS): wkappbot suggest resolve --confirm will say not-found for DGWCS entries -- this is a known bug (#19). Do not attempt workarounds.

suggest add-requirement: any contributor (not just maintainer) can add behavioral requirements to a pending suggest: wkappbot suggest add-requirement <ts> "cmd => expected" [--skill <id>]. --skill triggers immediate skill update. Skill copy-on-edit: skill edit auto-forks HQ-only skills into caller's ProjectSkillsDir so suggesters can contribute without manual copy.

REPRO SKILL WORKFLOW: 1) wkappbot skill contribute --id <id> --app <app> --title "..." --desc "..." --steps "..." --requirement "wkappbot ... => ..." (x3 min)  2) wkappbot skill install  (HQ required -- resolvers across repos must reach it)  3) wkappbot suggest "title" --requirement ... --skill <id>. Guard checks: skill not found / not installed / HQ version older than project version -> each case gives a fix command.

requirements.prompt: smoke-help.ps1 glob pattern fixed. handlers/*.yml removed. yaml patterns correct.
